---
title: "Analysis of Cohort Studies III & IV"
format: 
  clean-typst:
    include-in-header: "custom.typ"
    commands: [small-cite]
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false    
author:
  - name: A Pathmeswaran
    orcid: 0000-0003-4065-2639
    email: pathmes@kln.ac.lk
date: 2026-04-06
date-format: long
bibliography: [static/references.bib]
bibliographystyle: "apa"
handout: false
---

```{r}
#| label: loadPacks

# library(ggdag)
library(tinytable)
library(epitools)
library(epiR)
# library(tidyverse)
# library(aod)
# library(summarytools)
# library(gt)
# library(gtsummary)
# suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(kableExtra))


```

### Syllabus

-   Confounding & Effect modification\
-   Stratified analysis
    -   calculation of pooled RR, IRR
    -   95% CI Mantel-Haenszel method
    -   Test for homogeneity

### Confounding

-   The phenomenon where the relationship between an exposure (E) and an outcome (O) is distorted by a third variable (confounder).
-   is associated with the outcome (causally) and the exposure (causally or non-causally).
-   is not an intermediate variable in the causal pathway E to O
-   The distortion could lead to
    -   an increase in the strength of association between E & O
    -   a decrease (even absence) in the strength of association between E & O
    -   a reversal in the direction of the association between E & O

### Confounding

```{r}
#| fig-width: 10
source(here::here("cohort2_graphs.R"))
render_graph(n_graph)


```

### Smoking and lung cancer

{{< v 3em >}}

[Smoking increases the risk of lung cancer manifold, but only a minority of smokers develop lung cancer!]{.alert}

### Effect Modification and Interaction

-   Effect Modification - Is the effect of exposure on outcome similar or not among subgroups based on other variabl/s?
    -   Homogeneity of effect
    -   Heterogeneity of effect
-   Interaction - The joint effect of two or more exposures on outcome.
    -   Synergistic
    -   Antagonistic

### NMR - Homogeneity/ Hetrogeneity of effect 

```{r}
tt(t66) |> 
  style_tt(i = c(1, 3) , background = "aqua") |> 
  style_tt(j = 3:4, align = "r")
```

-   Calculate and interpret AR and RR for maternal smoking/NMR association for the two strata defined by fathers' level of education.

### NMR - Interaction

```{r}
tt(t66) |> 
  style_tt(i = c(1) , background = "aqua")|> 
  style_tt(j = 3:4, align = "r") 
```

-   Calculate and interpret AR and RR for maternal smoking & fathers' education/NMR association using the first row as the reference category.
-   Joint effect
    -   Observed
    -   Expected

### PNMR - Homogeneity/ Hetrogeneity of effect

```{r}
tt(t67) |> 
  style_tt(i = c(1, 3) , background = "aqua") |> 
  style_tt(j = 3:4, align = "r")
```

-   Calculate and interpret AR and RR for maternal smoking/PNMR association for the two strata defined by fathers' level of education.

### PNMR - Interaction

```{r}
tt(t67) |> 
  style_tt(i = c(1) , background = "aqua") |> 
  style_tt(j = 3:4, align = "r")
```

-   Calculate and interpret AR and RR for maternal smoking & fathers' education/PNMR association using the first row as the reference category.
-   Joint effect
    -   Observed
    -   Expected


### Stratum Specific RR - NMR

F_Edu - 9p

```{r}
t66$deaths <- round(as.numeric(t66$NMR)*as.numeric(t66$L_Births)/1000)
t66$alive <- as.numeric(t66$L_Births)-t66$deaths
t66$births <- t66$deaths + t66$alive
# d <- with( t66 , aggregate( deaths , by=list(M_Smoke) , FUN=sum)  )
# b <- with( t66 , aggregate( births , by=list(M_Smoke) , FUN=sum)  )
# d$x*1000/b$x

m9p <- t66[c(1, 2),c(6, 5)]
mlt9 <- t66[c(3, 4),c(6, 5)]

rr9p <- riskratio.wald(as.matrix(m9p))

rrlt9 <- riskratio.wald(as.matrix(mlt9))

rr9p$measure


```

F_Edu lt9

```{r}
rrlt9$measure
```

### M-H Pooled RR vs Crude RR - NMR

```{r}
ma <- array(c(as.matrix(t66[c(2, 1),c(5, 6)]), as.matrix(t66[c(4, 3),c(5, 6)])), dim = c(2, 2, 2))
r2b2 <- epi.2by2(ma)
r2b2$massoc.summary[1:3, ] |> tt() |> 
 tt_format(j = 2:4, digits = 3)


```
### M-H Pooled RR Equation - Count data

::::: {layout-ncol="2"}
::: {#first-column}

| Stratum~`i`~ | Cases | N at risk |
|--------------|-------|-------------|
| Exposed      | a~1i~ | n~1i~       |
| Unexposed    | a~0i~ | n~0i~       |
| Total        |       | T~i~        |

:::

::: {#second-column}
![](rr_mhn.png)
:::
:::::


### M-H Pooled RR Equation - Person-time data

::::: {layout-ncol="2"}
::: {#first-column}

| Stratum~`i`~ | Cases | Person-time |
|--------------|-------|-------------|
| Exposed      | a~1i~ | y~1i~       |
| Unexposed    | a~0i~ | y~0i~       |
| Total        |       | T~i~        |

:::

::: {#second-column}
![](rr_mh.png)
:::
:::::

### Direct

```{r}
tt(t66) |> 
  style_tt(j = 3:7, align = "r")

d <- with( t66 , aggregate( deaths , by=list(F_Edu) , FUN=sum)  )
b <- with( t66 , aggregate( births , by=list(F_Edu) , FUN=sum)  )

print(paste("Births =", b[2]))
```
